mnemonics.title: Cut-Vertex Finding Rule
mnemonics.content: Root: 2+ children = articulation. Non-root: child with low ≥ disc = articulation. (Think: 'Root has 2 kids, non-root's child can't fly high enough')

mnemonics.title: Connectivity Inequality
mnemonics.content: κ ≤ λ ≤ δ (kappa-lambda-delta). Remember: K comes before L before D alphabetically, same as the inequality.

mnemonics.title: Block Properties
mnemonics.content: Blocks share cut-vertices, not edges: 'Blocks hug at articulation points, never share edges'

acronyms.usage: Used to find cut-vertices and bridges in O(V+E) time using discovery time and low values
Can be used for finding shortest paths in unweighted graphs, not typically for cut-vertices
Minimum vertices to disconnect graph; κ(Kn) = n-1
Minimum edges to disconnect graph; λ(G) ≤ δ(G)
In Tarjan's algorithm, low[v] = min(disc[v], disc[w]) for all back edges and tree edges

visualTips.visualization: Draw DFS tree with discovery times. Mark each vertex's low value. For non-root: if any child has low ≥ disc[parent], parent is articulation. For root: if ≥2 children, articulation.
List all cut-vertices in one column, all blocks in another. Connect each cut-vertex to all blocks containing it. Result is always a tree.
A graph is biconnected if: (1) connected, (2) no cut-vertices, (3) any two vertices lie on a common cycle.

analogies.analogy: Like a hub airport - if it closes, destinations become disconnected from each other.
Like a single bridge connecting two islands - if removed, islands become separated.
Like a room in a house - each room is biconnected, doors (cut-vertices) connect rooms.
Minimum number of players to remove to break the team into separate groups.
